info.description: Unified social media inbox API. Read and respond to comments, DMs, reviews, and mentions across Instagram, Facebook, Threads, Google Business Profile, TikTok, and LinkedIn through a single REST API. YouTube, X/Twitter, and Trustpilot coming soon.
info.title: SocialAPI.AI

get.description: Returns all active social media accounts connected to the authenticated API key's workspace.

post.description:
Initiates account connection. For OAuth2 platforms returns an auth_url to redirect the user to.
For apikey/credentials platforms stores the credentials immediately and returns account_id.

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a connected account. It will no longer appear in the accounts list
and all API calls using this account will return 404.

get.description: Returns platform-specific quota data for a connected account as a key/value map where values are remaining counts. Keys are platform-defined — for example Instagram returns `posts_remaining`. An empty object is returned for platforms without usage limits.

501.description: Platform does not expose usage limits

get.description: Fetches posts and stories where your brand account was mentioned or tagged. Results are sorted newest-first. Returns 501 for platforms that do not support mention tracking.

parameters.description: Return only items created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

501.description: Platform does not support mention tracking

get.description: Returns all brands for the authenticated user with their connected account counts.

post.description: Creates a new brand for grouping connected social accounts. Each brand can have one account per platform.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing name

delete.description: Deletes a brand and disconnects all its connected accounts.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing name

get.description: Returns inbox posts that have comments, ordered by most-recently-commented. Supports filtering by account, platform, minimum comment count, and date range. Uses cursor-based pagination.

parameters.description: Filter by platform (instagram, facebook, threads, tiktok, linkedin, google)
parameters.name: platform

parameters.description: Only return posts with at least this many comments (default 0)
parameters.name: min_comments

parameters.description: Only return posts commented after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

get.description: Returns comments for a specific inbox post, including per-platform capability flags (can_reply, can_delete, can_hide, can_like, can_private_reply). Uses cursor-based pagination.

parameters.description: Connected account ID that owns the post
parameters.name: account_id

200.description: Paginated list of comments with capabilities

post.description: Posts a reply on the platform. If comment_id is provided, replies to that specific comment (threaded reply); otherwise replies to the post itself (top-level comment). Consumes 1 interaction credit.

501.description: Platform does not support comment replies

delete.description: Deletes a comment on the platform and removes it from the inbox. Not all platforms support comment deletion — check the can_delete capability flag.

501.description: Platform does not support comment deletion

post.description: Hides a comment on the platform so it is not visible to the public, and marks it hidden in the inbox. Supported on Instagram, Facebook, and Threads. Check the can_hide capability flag.

501.description: Platform does not support hiding comments

delete.description: Restores a previously hidden comment so it is publicly visible again. Supported on Instagram, Facebook, and Threads.

501.description: Platform does not support hiding comments

post.description: Likes a comment on the platform as the connected account. Supported on Instagram, Facebook, Twitter/X, and Reddit. Check the can_like capability flag.

501.description: Platform does not support comment likes

delete.description: Removes a like from a comment on the platform. Supported on Instagram, Facebook, Twitter/X, and Reddit.

501.description: Platform does not support comment likes

post.description: Sends a direct message to the author of a comment. This is a one-time DM triggered by a specific comment — only supported on Instagram and Facebook. Check the can_private_reply capability flag.
post.summary: Send a private reply to a commenter

501.description: Platform does not support private replies

get.description: Returns paginated DM conversations for the authenticated user, optionally filtered by account, platform, and status.
Returns a single inbox conversation by ID.

patch.description: Sets the status of a conversation to active or archived.

400.description: Invalid request body or status value

get.description: Returns paginated messages for an inbox conversation, newest first.

post.description: Sends an outgoing DM via the connected platform account and records it in the conversation.
post.summary: Send a message in a conversation

400.description: Invalid request body or missing required fields

501.description: Platform does not support sending DMs

post.description: Resets the unread count on a conversation to zero.

get.description: Fetches reviews across connected accounts via real-time fan-out to platform APIs. Supports filtering by account and platform. Reviews are proxied live and not stored — results may vary based on platform availability. Currently only Google Business Profile returns reviews.

parameters.description: Only return reviews created after this timestamp (RFC3339)
parameters.name: since

put.description: Updates the text of an existing review reply. Not yet implemented — returns 501.

post.description: Posts a public reply to a review on the platform (e.g. Google Business Profile). The reply is visible to anyone who views the review. Consumes 1 interaction credit.

501.description: Platform does not support review replies

delete.description: Deletes an existing review reply from the platform. Not yet implemented — returns 501.

get.description:
Public endpoint (no auth). Validates the invite token and redirects the user
to the platform's OAuth authorization page. On successful OAuth callback,
the account is connected to the invite creator's brand.
Returns all invites (active and used/expired) for a brand, ordered by created_at DESC.

post.description:
Creates a single-use invite link for an external user to connect a social account to a brand.
The brand must not already have a connected account for the specified platform, and
no active (unused, unexpired) invite may exist for the same brand+platform combination.

409.description: Account or active invite already exists

delete.description: Marks an invite as used (revoked), preventing it from being redeemed.

404.description: Invite not found or already used

get.description: Returns all API keys for the authenticated user.

post.description: Generates a new API key for the authenticated user.

400.description: Invalid request body or missing name

delete.description: Deactivates an API key. This is irreversible.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of uploaded media files for the authenticated user.
Returns total bytes used, the plan limit, and file count for the authenticated user.

post.description: Upload a media file directly through the API server.

get.description:
Returns a presigned S3 PUT URL for client-side media upload. Upload the file directly
via HTTP PUT to the returned URL, then call POST /v1/media/:id/verify to confirm.
get.summary: Get a presigned media upload URL

post.description: Confirms that a presigned-URL upload completed successfully and marks the media as ready.

Exchanges an authorization code (received from the platform callback) for tokens.
Validates the state token against Redis to prevent CSRF. State is one-time-use.

403.description: State belongs to a different API key

get.description: Returns posts with rich filtering and cursor pagination. Supports filtering by status, platform, account_ids, date range, and text search.

parameters.description: Filter by status (draft, scheduled, publishing, published, partial, failed, cancelled)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Sort order: scheduled_asc (default), scheduled_desc, created_asc, created_desc
parameters.name: sort

post.description:
Publishes to one or more connected accounts. If scheduled_at is omitted the post is
published immediately (synchronous — blocks until all accounts respond). If scheduled_at
is set the post is queued for future publication (asynchronous). Omit account_ids and
scheduled_at to create a draft. Consumes 1 post credit.

201.description: All accounts published (sync) or post scheduled/drafted

207.description: Some accounts published, some failed (sync only)

post.description: Upload a CSV file to create multiple posts. Use dry_run=true to validate without creating.

get.description: Returns media and text constraints for all supported platforms. Cacheable.

post.description: Dry-run validation. Returns errors for hard limit violations and warnings for best-practice issues.

get.description: Fetch a post by its ID with all platform delivery details.

delete.description: Cancels a scheduled post or deletes a failed/published post across all platforms.

patch.description:
Updates the text, media, schedule time, targets, or hidden status of a pending post.
Only posts in `draft`, `scheduled`, or `failed` status can be updated.

get.description: Fetches live engagement metrics from platform APIs for a published post.

post.description: Re-attempts publication of a post in `failed` or `partial` status. Consumes 1 post credit.
Unpublishes a published post from a specific account (if account_id provided) or all accounts.

get.description: Full-text search across all platforms and interaction types. Accepts query param `q` for the search term; optionally filter by `platform` and `type`. Fan-out to all relevant connectors with merged, ranked results. **Not yet implemented** — returns 501.

Returns brands, posts, and interactions consumed during the current billing period alongside the plan limits. -1 means unlimited (all paid plans). Period boundaries come from Stripe subscription or rolling 30-day windows for free users.
get.summary: Get resource usage for the current billing period

200.description: Resource usage for the current billing period

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile (id, email, plan tier, onboarding flag).

delete.description: Permanently deletes the authenticated user account and all associated data (API keys, connected accounts, usage logs). This action is irreversible.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's profile. Currently only supports setting onboarding=false to mark onboarding as complete.

400.description: Invalid request body or onboarding value

post.description: Enqueue an async analytics export job for a connected account. Subject to plan limits (exports per month, cooldown hours, concurrent export cap).

get.description: Returns the 50 most recent export jobs for the authenticated user. Optionally filter by status.

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, processing, completed, failed)
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns the current status, progress, result download URL (presigned Excel), and report URL for an export job.
Returns all webhook endpoints for the authenticated user.

post.description: Registers a new webhook endpoint. The generated secret is returned once — store it securely.

delete.description: Removes a webhook endpoint. Future events will not be delivered to this URL.

patch.description: Partially updates a webhook endpoint. All fields are optional; at least one must be provided.

BearerAuth.description: Prefix your API key with "Bearer ". Example: `Authorization: Bearer sapi_key_...`
BearerAuth.name: Authorization

brand_id.description: BrandID optionally specifies which brand to attach the account to. If omitted, a new brand is auto-created.

metadata.description: Metadata contains platform-specific connection data (e.g. redirect_uri for OAuth2 platforms).

platform.description: Platform identifies the social platform to connect (e.g. instagram, facebook, google, threads, tiktok, linkedin).

message.example: Store this key securely. It will not be shown again.

first_comment.description: FirstComment is auto-posted as the first comment after publishing (Instagram, LinkedIn).

media_ids.description: MediaIDs are previously uploaded media file IDs to attach.

publish_now.description: PublishNow immediately publishes if targets are provided (ignores scheduled_at).

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt queues the post for future publication. Omit to publish immediately or create a draft.

skip_duplicate_check.description: SkipDuplicateCheck bypasses the duplicate content detection.

targets.description: Targets specifies which connected accounts to publish to, with optional per-target overrides.

text.description: Text is the post body content. Required.
text.example: Check out our new product launch! #newproduct

title.description: Title is an optional post title (used by LinkedIn, Google).

visibility.description: Visibility controls who can see the post. Platform-dependent values: public, private, connections_only.

events.description: Events is the list of event types to subscribe to.

url.description: URL is the HTTPS endpoint that will receive webhook POST requests.

message.example: Store the secret securely. It will not be shown again.

code.description: Code is the authorization code received from the platform callback.

metadata.description: Metadata must include redirect_uri and state for CSRF validation.

platform.description: Platform identifies the social platform (must match the connect call).

text.example: Hi, I have a question about your product

use_case.example: Managing my own brand's social accounts

text.example: Check out our new product launch!

message.example: The post exceeds the character limit for this platform

account_id.description: AccountID is the connected account to publish to. Required.

first_comment.description: FirstComment overrides the post-level first comment for this target.

media_ids.description: MediaIDs overrides the post-level media for this target.

platform_data.description: PlatformData passes platform-specific fields (e.g. TikTok privacy settings, LinkedIn article metadata).

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt overrides the post-level schedule time for this target.

text.description: Text overrides the post-level text for this target.

title.description: Title overrides the post-level title for this target.

visibility.description: Visibility overrides the post-level visibility for this target.

account_id.description: AccountID optionally scopes the unpublish to a single platform target.

hidden.description: Hidden archives the post from default list views when true.

media_ids.description: MediaIDs replaces the attached media files.

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt changes the scheduled publication time (RFC3339).

targets.description: Targets replaces or updates per-platform target configuration.

text.description: Text replaces the post body. Only for draft/scheduled/failed posts.

visibility.description: Visibility changes who can see the post.

events.description: Events replaces the subscribed event types.

is_active.description: IsActive enables or disables delivery to this endpoint.

url.description: URL changes the webhook delivery endpoint. Must be HTTPS.

account_ids.description: AccountIDs lists connected account IDs — their platforms are resolved automatically.

media_ids.description: MediaIDs are media file IDs to validate (checks count and type limits).

platforms.description: Platforms lists platform names to validate against (e.g. ["instagram", "linkedin"]).

scheduled_at.description: ScheduledAt validates the schedule time (must be in the future, within platform limits).

targets.description: Targets validates per-target overrides if provided.

text.description: Text is the post content to validate against platform constraints.

text.example: Love this product! Really glad I found it.
